$schema-note: Policy for scripts/check-licenses.mjs (#462). `allowed` lists SPDX identifiers permitted anywhere in the shipped closure; a package passes if its license expression is satisfiable using only allowed ids (OR = any branch, AND = every operand). `exceptions` are reviewed grants: for packages whose name matches `packages` (exact, or a trailing `*` prefix), the ids in `allow` (or the synthetic `UNKNOWN` token) are additionally permitted. Each needs a reason. Keep both tight.

exceptions.reason: libvips prebuilt shared libraries (all platform variants), loaded dynamically by sharp as separate .node/.dylib/.dll binaries. LGPL permits distribution inside an MIT app provided its license text ships (THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.md) and the library can be relinked — satisfied because each ships as a replaceable standalone binary, not statically linked.
Windows sharp binaries statically embed libvips, so npm declares `Apache-2.0 AND LGPL-3.0-or-later`. Same LGPL basis as the libvips prebuilts above; Apache-2.0 is already allowlisted.
WASM sharp binary declares `Apache-2.0 AND LGPL-3.0-or-later AND MIT`. Same LGPL basis as the libvips prebuilts; Apache-2.0 and MIT are already allowlisted.
